heading: Governing body
label: 220104 Governing body
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)220104(a)36:1305(b), (c)., –(d), .Dec. 29, 1979Pub. L. 96–165, § 5(b)93 Stat. 1270220104(b)36:1305(d).In subsection (a)(1), the words “The board of governors is the governing body of the corporation” are substituted for “The management of the corporation shall be entrusted to a board of governors” for consistency in the revised title.In subsection (a)(2), the text of 36:1305(b) (2d sentence) and the word “Thereafter” are omitted as executed and obsolete.In subsection (b)(1), the word “offered” is substituted for “tendered” to use a more common word.

L. 96–165, § 5(b)93 Stat. 1270220104(b)36:1305(d).In subsection (a)(1), the words “The board of governors is the governing body of the corporation” are substituted for “The management of the corporation shall be entrusted to a board of governors” for consistency in the revised title.In subsection (a)(2), the text of 36:1305(b) (2d sentence) and the word “Thereafter” are omitted as executed and obsolete.In subsection (b)(1), the word “offered” is substituted for “tendered” to use a more common word.

children.text: The board of governors is the governing body of the corporation. The board is responsible for the general policies and program of the corporation and for the control of the affairs and property of the corporation.

children.label: A
children.text: six members appointed by the President;

children.label: B
children.text: the Secretary of Defense or the Secretary’s designee; and

children.label: C
children.text: representatives of the civilian organizations listed in , and of the public at large, as provided in the bylaws.section 220102(4) of this title
The corporation may have other governing bodies or committees as provided in the bylaws.

The office of honorary chairman of the corporation shall be offered to the President. On acceptance, the honorary chairman shall be invited to preside at meetings of the corporation that the honorary chairman deems appropriate and convenient.
The corporation may have other officers as provided in the bylaws.
